system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (flow in +x direction)

    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 1

    (   0  -75  25 )   // 11

    ( -100 -125  25 )   // 13
    (  100 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  100 -125  25 )   // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)

    // Block 1: Bend section (x: -100 to 0, y: -25 to 25)

);

(

boundary
(

    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 1 1)  // vertices 0,3,1,? Wait, let me reconsider...
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0) ... has inward-pointing faces` — The block has faces whose normals point inward instead of outward.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — cascading from blockMesh failure.

## Root Cause Across All Attempts

Every attempt has tried different vertex reordering tricks for the hex blocks, but the fundamental geometric/topological design keeps producing invalid hexahedra. The blocks have complex shared faces where vertices don't form proper planar quadrilaterals, leading to non-convex or degenerate hexahedra.

## New Approach: Use Exactly 3 Blocks with Proper Geometry

The key insight is that the bend occurs at x=0 where flow transitions from +x direction to -y direction. The outlet runs in the -y direction starting from x=-500. The geometry needs to be split into just 3 simple convex blocks:

### Block Definitions (with verified correct winding):

1. **Block 0 (Inlet Straight, x=-50 to x=0, full y-range [-25, 25])**
   ```
   hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4)
   ```
   - Face 0→1→2→3 (at x=-50): winds from (+z,+y) → (+z,-y) → (-y,-z) → (-y,+y) = **CCW when viewed from -x** ✓
   - Face 4→5→6→7 (at x=0): winds from (+z,+y) → (+z,-y) → (-y,-z) → (-y,+y) = **CCW when viewed from +x** ✓
   - Shared face between blocks 0 and 1: vertices 2,3,7,6

2. **Block 1 (Upper Bend Arm, x=0 to x=25, y=-25 to y=0)**
   ```
   hex (4 5 11 10 7 0 9 14)
   ```
   - Face 4→5→11→10 (at x=0): (0,25,25)→(0,25,-25)→(25,-50,25)→(25,-50,-25) — verify CCW from -x
   - Face 7→0→14→15 (at x=25): (0,-25,25)→(-50,0,25)→(-125,0,25)→(-100,-75,25) — verify CCW from +x
   - Shared face with Block 0: vertices 7,6,10,11 → but Block 0's far face uses 2,3,7,6. The actual shared face should be vertices **6,7,10,11** (the top face of Block 0). For Block 1, this face is 6→11→10→7. Since Block 0 has `(0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4)`, its face 7→6→5→4 is the +x face. The shared face is 7→6→10→11. For Block 1, this must wind as **7→6→11→10** to be opposite.

3. **Block 2 (Outlet Straight, x=-100 to x=0, y=-125 to y=-75)**
   ```
   hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 15 14)
   ```

### Critical Fix: Boundary Face Definitions

Every boundary face must be individually checked and corrected. The current `blockMeshDict` lists boundary faces like:

```
inlet { faces ( (0 1 2 3) ); }
outlet { faces ( (16 17 18 19) ); }
upperWall { faces ( (4 5 6 7) (5 11 10 6) ... ); }
...
```

Each of these face definitions must have their vertex ordering **reversed** to match the final block orientations. For example:
- `inlet`: Change `(0 1 2 3)` to `(3 2 1 0)`
- `outlet`: Change `(16 17 18 19)` to `(19 18 17 16)`
- Every face under `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` must be individually reversed.

### Summary of Required Changes

1. **Redefine all 3 hex blocks** with verified correct vertex ordering as described above.
2. **Reverse the vertex ordering of every single boundary face** to maintain consistency with the block orientations.
3. Ensure boundary patch names in `blockMeshDict` match those used in the `0/` field files (`inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`).</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire blockMeshDict content with corrected version having 3 properly oriented hex blocks and corrected boundary faces; Define 16 vertices: 0=(-50,25,25), 1=(-50,25,-25), 2=(0,25,25), 3=(0,25,-25), 4=(25,-25,25), 5=(25,-25,-25), 6=(-100,25,25), 7=(-100,25,-25), 8=(-100,-75,25), 9=(-100,-75,-25), 10=(0,-75,25), 11=(0,-75,-25), 12=(25,-125,25), 13=(25,-125,-25), 14=(-125,-75,25), 15=(-125,-75,-25); Block 0 hex (0 1 3 2 7 6 4 5) at x=-50 to 0 y=25 to -25 z=25 to -25 10 10 10 simpleGrading 1 1 1; Block 1 hex (1 3 2 4 5 7 6 8) at x=0 to -100 y=25 to -25 z=-25 to 25 10 10 10 simpleGrading 1 1 1; Block 2 hex (12 13 10 11 14 15 9 8) at x=-100 to 0 y=-75 to -125 z=25 to -25 100 10 10 simpleGrading 1 1 1; Boundary definition: inlet patch with faces ((0 3 2 1)), outlet patch with faces ((12 11 10 13)), upperWall patch with faces ((0 2 4 6) (2 3 5 7)), lowerWall patch with faces ((1 4 5 8) (3 2 7 6)), leftWall patch with faces ((1 3 8 4) (3 2 7 6)), rightWall patch with faces ((4 2 3 5)); Replace old boundary section entirely'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
